markdownRemark.html:
Goal:

  • Creating an npm package that can be installed through npm or yarn from the npm registry

Steps:

  • Setup npm account if you don't have one
  • Create new local directory

  • Create package.json with touch package.json

    • Add stuff into your package.json

      {
"name": [@username]/[package_name],
"version": "1.0.0"

    {

  • All you need is the name and version in order to publish with npm publish --access=public
  • Create GitHub repo

  • Add license from GitHub

    • Insights => Community => Add License
  • Add some actual code into index.js

  • Add more stuff to package.json

    {
  "description": ...
  "license": ...
  "repository" ...
  "main": index.js,
  "keywords": []
}

  • Add shields from shields.io

    • Use npm (scoped) and npm bundle size (scoped)
  • Update versions with npm version major
  • Do npm publish again

Resources

  • https://www.freecodecamp.org/news/how-to-make-a-beautiful-tiny-npm-package-and-publish-it-2881d4307f78/
  • https://github.com/flexdinesh/npm-module-boilerplate
